worker.notes: Required OpenCode GLM-5.1 implementation run made useful bounded edits but the command timed out before final summary. Hermes reviewed, fixed a mock-interface compile issue, tightened repository error handling/locking, and completed validation directly.

summary: Added persistent persona profile storage for book repositories. Persona profiles can be saved, loaded, and merged from segment personas into books/<bookID>/personas.json with per-book locking, stable persona/display fields, aggregate voice descriptions, segment counts, and deterministic output ordering.

tests.command: opencode run --model opencode-go/glm-5.1 --format json <bounded implementation task>
tests.notes: OpenCode used exactly the required model; it reported passing Go tests using an internal path override, but the supervisor environment has no go binary and the OpenCode command timed out before final completion.

Review returned revise due swallowed storage errors and idempotency/test-gap notes. Hermes fixed swallowed storage errors and added invalid JSON coverage. Idempotency remains documented as net-new segment merge semantics for this narrow slice.
Expo web export passed with existing expo-av deprecation warning.
The go binary is not installed in this Hermes environment; OpenCode reported an internal go run, but Hermes supervisor could not independently verify Go tests.

blockers: Go tests unavailable to Hermes because go is not installed.
risks: UpdatePersonaProfilesFromSegments is additive for net-new segment batches; if a caller replays the same batch, counts can inflate until segment-ID-level idempotency is added in a future task.
follow_up_suggestions: Hook UpdatePersonaProfilesFromSegments into segmentation pipeline after Go tests are available.
Consider segment-ID-level idempotency if pipeline replay semantics require it.
Run go test ./internal/book ./internal/pipeline on a Go 1.24.12 host.

questions: Are repository persona profiles persisted safely under books/<bookID>/personas.json?
Is additive segment merge acceptable for the first bounded KB slice?
